steps.title: Find the parabola maximum
steps.content:
The quadratic is in vertex form.
$$y=-3(x-23)^2+20$$
Because $-3<0$, the parabola opens downward, so its greatest possible $y$-value is $20$.

steps.title: Compare the horizontal line
steps.content: The other graph is the horizontal line $y=23$. Since $23$ is above the parabola's maximum value of $20$, the graphs do not intersect.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the two equations together. The horizontal line sits above the entire downward-opening parabola, so there are no intersection points. The answer is D.
